title: To Maurentius.

source.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: My most beloved son, Cyprian the deacon, had pleased me much by his return to me, if his whole self had returned to me.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But now that your Glory has stayed in Sicily, I know most certainly that he has returned indeed in body, but in mind has remained in Sicily.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Yet, in saying this, I rejoice with you for your quiet as much as I groan for my own occupations.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And to this I earnestly exhort you, that, if the pleasant savour of inward sweetness has touched the palate of your heart, your mind be so rapt within itself that all which sounds without, all that delights without, may be distasteful.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Moreover I commend you for avoiding concourses of men, seeing that a mind which desires to be renewed in God through the grace of compunction often relapses into its old state through evil conversation and words.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: I have sought for some to join you in a society for sacred reading, but have found no one, and I exceedingly lament the scarcity of what is good.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And though I, a sinner, am very much occupied, yet, if you should wish to come to the threshold of the blessed apostle Peter, you will be able to have me as a close associate in the study of Holy Writ.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: May Almighty God keep you under His heavenly protection, and grant you to remain defended against the snares of the ancient foe.
